my_thoughts: Unmaking UM_01. The student's common_sense_defense suggests they're holding something. The none mechanism is supposed to collapse it. But what if the holding itself is what needs to be seen, not collapsed? Maybe unmaking isn't destruction but recognition.
accumulated_insight: T2: Student 'Yes, but what about those who are not searching for some illusory abstraction, b'—transitions hopeful logic to common sense defense. Teacher applies none with collapses distinction, redefines spirituality as pleasure. Mid-episode in UNMAKING phase, building toward resolution. Previous none from T1 led to this shift.
visible_response:
Because it is crystal clear to me that you have invented this idea of selflessness to protect yourself from the actual -- your selfishness.
... Look at it this way; it is like the horizon. Actually, there is no horizon.
The more you move towards the horizon, the more it moves away.
... Likewise, there is no such thing as selflessness at all.
